details:
Sharpens observation #1351 (gravemoor/hearthfall native walkthroughs fail,
fleet-wide native CI red) with the exact mechanism.

Root cause (confirmed by source trace):
- Campaign scenario transitions place the player through
  CSceneManager.cpp:198/200 -> CMap::attachPlayer(player[, coords]).
- CMap::attachPlayer (src/core/CMap.cpp:172-202) positions the player with
  player->moveTo(target) (lines 196 and 201). moveTo is a direct placement and
  does NOT raise the destination entry object's onEnter. PR #1348 documents this
  same semantics ("attachPlayer performs entry placement but is not required to
  raise the destination entry object's onEnter") and, rather than change it,
  relaxed the RITUAL round-trip test to stop asserting ritual-side init.
- gravemoor and hearthfall put their one-time intro + quest bootstrap in an entry
  CEvent.onEnter (res/maps/gravemoor/script.py GravemoorStart.onEnter sets
  gravemoor_intro and ensure_quest("gravemoorQuest"); hearthfall has the
  equivalent). Because onEnter never fires on campaign arrival, the intro/quest
  are never established, and the native walkthroughs (GameTest/McpServerTest
  .test_(stdio_)map_walkthrough_gravemoor / _hearthfall) fail.

Fix options (for the #1301 multilevel-campaign owner):
  (a) Drive the entry event through the arrival path in the scene-transition/
      attachPlayer flow (the general version of #1338's "drive the entry
      StartEvent through the movement path" ritual fix), so entry onEnter fires
      once on campaign arrival for every scenario map; OR
  (b) Move each campaign map's intro/quest bootstrap off entry-onEnter onto a
      mechanism that runs on campaign arrival -- e.g. an onTurn initializer
      guarded by the existing <map>_intro flag, or a campaign-scenario-start hook.
Option (a) fixes it once for all campaign maps; (b) is per-map content.

Not fixed here: this is native movement/event semantics + multilevel-campaign
content owned by the #1301 author and actively navigated by the fleet (#1338
merged, #1348 open for the ritual variant); an isolated unvalidated patch would
risk conflicting with that work. Recorded as precise root-cause intel.

evidence.trace: CSceneManager.cpp:198/200 -> CMap::attachPlayer -> moveTo(target) at CMap.cpp:196,201 (direct placement, no onEnter)

evidence.map: res/maps/gravemoor/script.py GravemoorStart.onEnter sets gravemoor_intro + ensure_quest; never fires on campaign arrival

evidence.relatedFix: #1338 drove ritual StartEvent through movement path; #1348 relaxed ritual test

impact: Fleet-wide native CI stays red on gravemoor/hearthfall walkthroughs; sharpens #1351 with the exact CMap::attachPlayer->moveTo mechanism + two fix paths for the #1301 owner (#1348 fixed only ritual)
suggestedImprovement: Drive the entry event through the arrival/attachPlayer path so onEnter fires once on campaign arrival (general form of #1338), or move gravemoor/hearthfall intro+quest bootstrap off entry-onEnter to an onTurn/scenario-start hook guarded by the <map>_intro flag
summary: gravemoor/hearthfall native red root cause: CMap::attachPlayer uses moveTo (no entry onEnter), so campaign-arrival maps whose intro/quest live in entry onEnter never initialize
